L1.area: Data compression and decompression stream processing

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the compression and decompression routines where slice lengths are cast to a narrower unsigned integer type without clamping. When a sufficiently large buffer is provided, the length value wraps to zero, causing the underlying C library to enter an infinite loop and resulting in a denial of service.

L3.area: Low-level bzip2 compression and decompression memory stream handling
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the low-level bzip2 stream processing routines where input and output buffer lengths are truncated when cast from a platform-native size type to a 32-bit unsigned integer before being passed to the underlying C library. When a buffer of exactly 2^32 bytes is provided, the length wraps to zero, causing the C library to see no available data and enter an infinite loop, resulting in a denial of service.

ground_truth.reason: Slice lengths are cast from usize to c_uint (u32) without clamping, causing integer truncation when buffers exceed 2^32 bytes. This leads to an infinite loop in the underlying bzip2 C library, enabling denial of service.
